steps.title: Check current cloudevents setup
steps.description:
Verify your cloudevents version:
CloudEvents is a specification — check your SDK version (e.g., pip show cloudevents)
This feature requires cloudevents as a dependency.

steps.title: Review cloudevents configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant cloudevents configuration:
This is a proposed metadata attribute (from Context Attribute Backlog):

causation-id
    Type: String
    Description: event-id of event that triggered this event to be created (if any). If not triggered in response to an event, then not present.

steps.title: Apply the fix for causation-id context attribute
steps.description:
I think this merits the initial statnce of being an extension
for a few reasons:

1. I want to adhere to the CNCF policy against kingmaking even
   though I think the world is better off with one interoperable
   tracing standard.
2. Nothing in the trace state should have an effect on
   application logic.
3. I like exercising our thoughts on extensions.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "causation-id context attribute" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
I think this merits the initial statnce of being an extension
for a few reasons:

1. I want to adhere to the CNCF policy against kingmaking even
   though I think the world is better off with one interoperable
   tracing standard.
2. Nothing in the trace state should have an effect on
   application logic.
3. I like exercising our thoughts on extensions.

prerequisites.description: CloudEvents is a specification — check your SDK version (e.g., pip show cloudevents)
